synthesis.daily_theme: A ceasefire framework in Lebanon unlocks the Iran deal architecture — if Hezbollah lets it
synthesis.mechanism: Lebanon pilot zones framework → Hezbollah stand-down (contingent) → Iran restores ceasefire comms with US → Iran-US talks resume → Brent continues falling. Each link is contested. Hezbollah has already rejected the first step.

connections.cultural_response: Fleetwood Mac 'Landslide' NEW on chart; Everywhere +12; Kings of Leon +12; LINKIN PARK +13; Elton John +17; Radiohead +8
connections.mechanism: Specific song placements in finale soundtrack directly triggered streaming/purchase activity on Apple Music UK
connections.observation: The chart floods backward into late-80s/90s at the same moment UK Twitter splits over identity and belonging. 'Landslide' lands with particular weight when the present feels unstable. The audience knows what they're reaching for.

connections.macro_event: Digwa sentencing on George Floyd 6th anniversary
connections.cultural_response: UK Twitter: Farage, Sikh, Southport, Henry, Southampton, George Floyd all trending simultaneously
connections.mechanism: Sentencing date coinciding with BLM anniversary creates charged environment where right-wing mobilisation and counter-memory activate in the same moment
connections.observation: The UK culture war compressed into a single day's trending list. Neither side is engaging the other's actual argument. The political system is visibly unable to hold or mediate this.

connections.macro_event: Operation Southern Spear — 205 dead, minimal Western coverage
connections.cultural_response: Story absent from UK trending despite body count exceeding many covered conflicts
connections.mechanism: Geographic distance + counter-narcotics framing + alignment with major media's assumptions = near-silence
connections.observation: The absence is the signal. The Colombia election — directly implicating this — is 17 days away.
